parameters.query: =SELECT '{{ $json.sourceId }}'::text AS source_id, EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM seen_leads WHERE lead_id = '{{ $json.sourceId }}') AS already_seen;

INSERT INTO leads (lead_id, role_title, company, location, source_url, workplace, status, lead_score, breakdown, posting_age_days, campaign_label, description)
VALUES ($1,$2,$3,$4,$5,$6,'New',$7,$8,$9,$10,$11)
ON CONFLICT (lead_id) DO NOTHING
RETURNING lead_id;

options.queryReplacement: ={{ (() => { const d = $('Dedup and Score').all().find(x => String(x.json.sourceId) === String($json.source_id))?.json || {}; const cl = ($('Config').first()?.json?._campaignLabel || ''); return [ String(d.sourceId||''), d.roleTitle||d.jobTitle||'', d.company||'', d.location||'', d.sourceUrl||'', d.workplaceType||'', Number(d.leadScore||0), d.scoreBreakdown||'', (d.postingAgeDays==null?null:Number(d.postingAgeDays)), cl, d.fullDescription||'' ]; })() }}

={{ (() => { const d = $('Dedup and Score').all().find(x => String(x.json.sourceId) === String($json.source_id))?.json || {}; return [ String($json.source_id), String(d.source||'').toLowerCase() ]; })() }}

parameters.content:
# ⚡ Trigger
Form-driven. A recruiter submits the lead form
(niche roles, location, minimum posting age) and
this fires a one-off pull. No baked-in config.

# 🔍 Build & Send Search Query
Reads the recruiter form into a search config and
splits it into individual LinkedIn queries (with
run + budget guards). Uses the widest date window
on purpose, the goal is to surface OLD, still-open
postings, then rank them by age downstream.

# 🧹 Clean & Score (leads)
Flattens and normalizes the Apify results, dedups,
then DROPS anything younger than the staleness
floor and any staffing/recruiting firms. What
remains is scored as a lead: older posting +
hard-to-fill + on-niche + hiring-intent language
scores higher.

# 🗃️ Postgres Store (leads)
  **Two tables. `seen_leads` is the dedup ledger and the persistence signal: it tracks first_seen, last_seen, and times_seen, so a role that keeps reappearing across scans is a proven long-open lead. New leads land in `leads` with lead score, posting age, and campaign label; the front end filters and works them.**

# 📨 Outreach Package (Claude agents)
  **Hands the run off to the self-hosted FastAPI service. A Haiku agent reviews and tiers every new lead in Postgres automatically. Drafting stays manual: you pick the leads worth pursuing in the web UI, and a Sonnet agent writes a LinkedIn message + cold email referencing the specific long-open role, then stages them (per-campaign CSV/JSON) for your email tool or CRM. Nothing auto-sends.**

# 🔔 Slack Notify
Builds a summary of new leads found this run. If any
exist and SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL is set, it fires a Slack
alert to your channel. No results, no noise.

options.queryReplacement: ={{ (() => { const d = $('Dedup and Score').all().find(x => String(x.json.sourceId) === String($json.lead_id))?.json || {}; return [ String($json.lead_id), String(d.source||'').toLowerCase() ]; })() }}

parameters.content:
# 📊 Final Run Summary

**Builds a digest of the run (leads found, top by staleness, outreach drafts staged) and posts it to your Slack, so you can see the campaign's progress at a glance, straight from Postgres.**
